message:
feat(dirent): port __opendir / __closedir / __readdir / __readdir_r aliases (bd-uwax6)

Adds the four glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the
directory-iteration entry points:

- `__opendir` (delegates to `opendir`)
- `__closedir` (delegates to `closedir`)
- `__readdir` (delegates to `readdir`)
- `__readdir_r` (delegates to `readdir_r`)

glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the
symbols NSS modules and libc-internal lookup paths link against.
Without them, statically-linked NSS-using code fails at link
time.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public counterparts in dirent_abi — same
pattern as the previously-shipped __pthread_*, __wait* / __dup3
/ __pipe2 alias families.

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering: __opendir + __closedir
round-trip on /tmp; __readdir yields >= 2 entries (. + ..);
__readdir_r yields >= 2 entries with rc == 0 each call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
